title: linanthus androsaceus var. croceus
intro: Linanthus androsaceus var. croceus, also known as the Western Globemallow, is a plant species that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae) and is native to the western United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Linanthus androsaceus var. croceus include soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
